questions.question: What is the result of applying the law of idempotence (A) to a statement?
questions.explanation: The law of idempotence states that a statement and itself is always true.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is an example of the law of excluded middle?
options: A statement is true or false
A statement is neither true nor false
questions.explanation: The law of excluded middle states that a statement is either true or false, with no other possibilities.

questions.question: What happens when you apply the law of contrapositive to a statement?
options: The statement becomes equivalent to the original statement
questions.explanation: The law of contrapositive states that if p implies q, then not q implies not p.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of the law of non-contradiction?
options: A statement cannot be both true and false at the same time
A statement can be both true and false at the same time
A statement can only be true
A statement can only be false
questions.explanation: The law of non-contradiction states that a statement cannot be both true and false at the same time.

questions.question: What is the result of applying the law of excluded middle to a statement that is neither true nor false?
questions.explanation: The law of excluded middle states that a statement is either true or false, so a statement that is neither true nor false becomes false.
